text:
There once was a young girl named Melody who lived in a quaint village famous for its mysterious fog. Every morning was the same—it enveloped the streets, cloaked the fields, and hung around her bedroom clock. Despite this, Melody loved waking up to see the foggy landscape beyond her bedroom window.

One foggy morning, while counting clouds in her reflections, she noticed something unusual. A small figure was standing on the cliff edge overlooking this foggy valley. The figure seemed old, cloaked, and moved with a quiet dignity.

Melody approached, curious. "Hello?" she called out softly, fearing perhaps it was just a trick of the mind.

"Melody," said the old figure. "You've always seemed happy. Do you remember a bird?"

"I—yes, I know what you mean! There's a bird I've always wanted."

The old figure chuckled. "I guess I'm the bird you've wanted?"
